chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Bail Application No.S-655 of 2024 Date Order with Signature of Hon’ble Judge Hearing of bail application 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of bail application. 07.11.2024 Mr. Ajeebullah Junejo, Advocate for the Applicants Syed Zafar Ali Shah Bukhari, Advocae for the Complainant Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, DPG for the State O R D E R Zulfiqar Ali Sangi, J;- Applicants Dildar, Sarfraz, Niaz, Fayaz and Zohaib seek pre-arrest bail in FIR No.196 of 2024 registered under sections 506/2, 504, 114, 147, 148, 149, 337-F(v), 337- L(ii), 337-A(i) and 337-F(i) P.P.C at PS Pano Akil after their earlier bail for same relief was declined by learned Additional Sessions Judge, Pano Akil vide impugned order dated 20.09.2024, hence this bail application. 2. The facts relating to bail application in hand are already mentioned in FIR as well as memo, therefore, there is no need to reproduce the same. 3. The allegation of causing injuries to complainant party is alleged against the applicants, however, today complainant, namely Ghulam Murtaza is present and states that they have patched up with the applicants outside of the Court and raised no objection for confirmation of the bail to the applicants. 4. Learned DPG for the State in view of the compromise between the applicants and the complainant, has also conceded for confirmation of bail to the applicants. 5. Heard learned counsel for the applicants, learned Counsel for the complainant and DPG for the State and perused the material available on record. It reflects that the offence in which the applicants are involved carries punishment upto 05 years, hence, does not fall within the prohibitory clause of Section 497

Heard learned counsel for the applicants, learned Counsel for the complainant and DPG for the State and perused the material available on record. It reflects that the offence in which the applicants are involved carries punishment upto 05 years, hence, does not fall within the prohibitory clause of Section 497 Page 2 of 2 CrPC. Further the parties have patched up their dispute outside of the Court and complainant has raised no objection for grant of bail to the applicants. In view of the above the interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicants vide order dated 23.09.2024 is hereby confirmation on same terms and conditions. 6. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. Judge ARBROHI
